sub_task: Verify launcher model handling and rotation
overall_summary: bin/stampede.sh supports --model and comma-separated --models rotation for regular workers and metaswarm commanders; assigned models are passed to Copilot CLI/custom agent commands and recorded in fleet.json. The requested premium model names are preserved in metaswarm policy pools/prompts. Main risk: model strings are not normalized or allowlist-validated; whitespace or empty comma entries can create invalid model slots, and preflight validation checks only the single --model fallback path in one code path.

workers.conclusion: PASS with caveat. --model is parsed into MODEL and defaults to claude-haiku-4.5; it is used as fallback when --models is absent. Runtime command generation passes the selected worker_model with --model to copilot/gh copilot, and custom --agent-cmd can substitute {model}. Preflight/access testing has a path that uses MODEL directly rather than rotated MODEL_LIST.

--models is a comma-separated string parsed with IFS=, read -ra MODEL_LIST and Python split(,). It rotates round-robin and overrides --model via ${MODELS:-$MODEL}. Whitespace and empty entries are not trimmed/filtered, so invalid model values can propagate.

Metaswarm commanders use 1-based round-robin assignment m=models[(i-1)%len(models)] / MODEL_LIST[(worker_num-1)%MODEL_COUNT]. Commander panes use stampede-commander and pass --model worker_model to Copilot CLI. No off-by-one issue found.

fleet.json is written with per-worker/per-commander model fields. Regular workers get {model, slot, role}; metaswarm commanders get model plus profile, premium model policy, progress_dir, telemetry contract, and premium_model_pool containing the requested premium models exactly.

RISK. No static allowlist validation for --model/--models was found in the launcher. Model availability is inferred from Copilot output during preflight/access checks by grepping for invalid/not found/not available, and only reports MODEL in that check. The launcher otherwise passes arbitrary model strings through.

specific_checks.--model_handling: Parsed at bin/stampede.sh:35 into MODEL with default at line 15; used as fallback for MODEL_LIST at line 605 and Python fleet models at line 637; selected worker_model is passed to --model in generated Copilot commands at lines 940-958.
specific_checks.--models_array_parsing: Comma-separated only, via IFS=, read -ra at line 605 and .split(,) at line 637; no trimming/filtering; --models takes precedence over --model through ${MODELS:-$MODEL}.
specific_checks.commander_assignment: Metaswarm uses commander ids commander-%03d and assigns model m = models[(i - 1) % len(models)] at lines 721-730; generated panes call stampede-commander with --model worker_model.
specific_checks.fleet_json: Written at lines 842-843; model field included for both workers and commanders; requested premium models appear exactly in premium_model_pool at lines 743-747 and 797-801.
specific_checks.model_validation: No allowlist/normalization found. Only preflight output heuristic at lines 448-455; custom agent commands skip automated access/model validation path.

risks: Whitespace in --models entries is preserved (for example "a, b" yields " b").
Empty --models entries are not rejected before rotation/fleet writing.
No launcher allowlist guarantees that only supported premium models are used; unsupported values are delegated to Copilot CLI failure handling.
One preflight model-availability path reports/checks MODEL rather than every rotated model in MODELS.
recommendations: Normalize --models by trimming whitespace and rejecting empty entries before MODEL_COUNT is used.
Consider validating every requested model against an explicit supported/premium allowlist or perform per-model Copilot preflight checks.
Align preflight model availability messages/checks with rotated MODEL_LIST rather than only MODEL.
